classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.ScfgwOp
documentation:
Write the value in rs1 to the Snitch stream configuration
location pointed by rs2 in the memory-mapped address space.

This is a RISC-V ISA extension, part of the `Xssr' extension.
https://pulp-platform.github.io/snitch/rm/custom_instructions/

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.ScfgwiOp
documentation:
Write the value in rs to the Snitch stream configuration location pointed by
immediate value in the memory-mapped address space.

This is a RISC-V ISA extension, part of the `Xssr' extension.
https://pulp-platform.github.io/snitch/rm/custom_instructions/

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FRepOperation

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FRepOperation.max_inst
documentation: Number of instructions to be repeated.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FRepOperation.max_rep
documentation: Number of times to repeat the instructions.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FRepOperation.body
documentation: Instructions to repeat, containing maximum 15 instructions, with no side effects.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FRepOperation.iter_args
documentation: Loop-carried variable initial values.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FRepOperation.stagger_mask
documentation:
4 bits for each operand (rs1 rs2 rs3 rd). If the bit is set, the corresponding operand
is staggered.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FRepOperation.stagger_count
documentation:
3 bits, indicating for how many iterations the stagger should increment before it
wraps again (up to 23 = 8).

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FRepOperation.res
documentation: Loop-carried variable initial values.

From the Snitch paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2002.10143

The frep instruction marks the beginning of a floating-point kernel which should be
repeated. It indicates how many subsequent instructions are stored in the sequence
buffer, how often and how (operand staggering, repetition mode) each instruction is
going to be repeated.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FrepOuterOp
documentation:
Repeats the instruction in the body as if the body were the body of a for loop, for
example:

```
# Repeat 4 times, stagger 1, period 2
li a0, 4
frep.o a0, 2, 1, 0b1010
fadd.d fa0, ft0, ft2
fmul.d fa0, ft3, fa0
```

is equivalent to:
```
fadd.d fa0, ft0, ft2
fmul.d fa0, ft3, fa0
fadd.d fa1, ft0, ft3
fmul.d fa1, ft3, fa1
fadd.d fa0, ft0, ft2
fmul.d fa0, ft3, fa0
fadd.d fa1, ft0, ft3
fmul.d fa1, ft3, fa1
```

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.FrepInnerOp
documentation:
Repeats the instruction in the body, as if each were in its own body of a for loop,
for example:

```
# Repeat three times, stagger 2, period 2
li a0, 3
frep.i a0, 2, 2, 0b0100
fadd.d fa0, ft0, ft2
fmul.d fa0, ft3, fa0
```

is equivalent to:
```
fadd.d fa0, ft0, ft2
fadd.d fa0, ft1, ft3
fadd.d fa0, ft2, ft3
fmul.d fa0, ft3, fa0
fmul.d fa0, ft4, fa0
fmul.d fa0, ft5, fa0
```

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.DMSourceOp
documentation: endregion
region XDMA extensions
Documentation for these operations:
https://pulp-platform.github.io/snitch_cluster/rm/custom_instructions.html

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.VFCpkASSOp
documentation:
Packs two scalar f32 values from rs1 and rs2 and packs the result as two adjacent
entries into the vectorial 2xf32 rd operand, such as:

f[rd][lo] = f[rs1]
f[rd][hi] = f[rs2]
endregion
region Snitch Packed SIMD Extension
Operations that map directly to the packed SIMD ISA provided by Snitch FPU.
The implemented ISA is *almost* the one specified here:
* https://iis-git.ee.ethz.ch/smach/smallFloat-spec/-/blob/master/smallFloat_isa.pdf
Beware of main undocumented differences from the spec:
* Additional reductions (e.g.: vfsum.*)
* Missing reductions (e.g.: vfdotp.*)
* Control of alternative FP formats (e.g.: IEEE fp16 vs BF16) delegated to the
RISC-V float CSR instead of being part of the encoding

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.VFMulSOp
documentation:
Performs vectorial multiplication of corresponding f32 values from
rs1 and rs2 and stores the results in the corresponding f32 lanes
into the vectorial 2xf32 rd operand, such as:

f[rd][lo] = f[rs1][lo] * f[rs2][lo]
f[rd][hi] = f[rs1][hi] * f[rs2][hi]

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.VFAddSOp
documentation:
Performs vectorial addition of corresponding f32 values from
rs1 and rs2 and stores the results in the corresponding f32 lanes
into the vectorial 2xf32 rd operand, such as:

f[rd][lo] = f[rs1][lo] + f[rs2][lo]
f[rd][hi] = f[rs1][hi] + f[rs2][hi]

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.VFAddHOp
documentation:
Performs vectorial addition of corresponding f16 values from
rs1 and rs2 and stores the results in the corresponding f16 lanes
into the vectorial 4xf16 rd operand, such as:

f[rd][0] = f[rs1][0] + f[rs2][0]
f[rd][1] = f[rs1][1] + f[rs2][1]
f[rd][2] = f[rs1][2] + f[rs2][2]
f[rd][3] = f[rs1][3] + f[rs2][3]

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.VFMaxSOp
documentation:
Performs vectorial maximum of corresponding f32 values from
rs1 and rs2 and stores the results in the corresponding f32 lanes
into the vectorial 2xf32 rd operand, such as:

f[rd][lo] = max(f[rs1][lo], f[rs2][lo])
f[rd][hi] = max(f[rs1][hi], f[rs2][hi])

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.RdRsRsAccumulatingFloatOperationWithFastMath
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have one destination floating-point register,
that also acts as a source register, and two source floating-point registers and can
be annotated with fastmath flags.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.RdRsAccumulatingFloatOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have one destination floating-point register,
that also acts as a source register, and a source floating-point register.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.VFMacSOp
documentation:
Performs vectorial multiplication of corresponding f32 values from
rs1 and rs2 and accumulates the results in the corresponding f32 lanes
into the vectorial 2xf32 rd operand, such as:

f[rd][lo] = f[rs1][lo] * f[rs2][lo] + f[rd][lo]
f[rd][hi] = f[rs1][hi] * f[rs2][hi] + f[rd][hi]

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv_snitch.VFSumSOp
documentation:
Performs sum of f32 values from rs and accumulates the result in the lower f32 value
of the rd operand:

f[rd][lo] = f[rs][hi] + f[rs][lo] + f[rd][lo]

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RISCVInstruction.comment
documentation: An optional comment that will be printed along with the instruction.

Base class for operations that can be a part of RISC-V assembly printing. Must
represent an instruction in the RISC-V instruction set, and have the following format:

name arg0, arg1, arg2           # comment

The name of the operation will be used as the RISC-V assembly instruction name.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsRsOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have one destination register, and two source
registers.

This is called R-Type in the RISC-V specification.
endregion
region Base Operation classes

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsRsFloatOperationWithFastMath
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have one destination floating-point register,
and two source floating-point registers and can be annotated with fastmath flags.

This is called R-Type in the RISC-V specification.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdImmIntegerOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have one destination register, and one
immediate operand (e.g. U-Type and J-Type instructions in the RISC-V spec).

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdImmJumpOperation

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdImmJumpOperation.rd
documentation:
The rd register here is not a register storing the result, rather the register where
the program counter is stored before jumping.

In the RISC-V spec, this is the same as `RdImmOperation`. For jumps, the `rd` register
is neither an operand, because the stored value is overwritten, nor a result value,
because the value in `rd` is not defined after the jump back. So the `rd` makes the
most sense as an attribute.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsImmIntegerOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have one destination register, one source
register and one immediate operand.

This is called I-Type in the RISC-V specification.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsImmShiftOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have one destination register, one source
register and one immediate operand.

This is called I-Type in the RISC-V specification.

Shifts by a constant are encoded as a specialization of the I-type format.
The shift amount is encoded in the lower 5 bits of the I-immediate field for RV32

For RV32I, SLLI, SRLI, and SRAI generate an illegal instruction exception if
imm[5] 6 != 0 but the shift amount is encoded in the lower 6 bits of the I-immediate field for RV64I.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsImmJumpOperation

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsImmJumpOperation.rd
documentation:
The rd register here is not a register storing the result, rather the register where
the program counter is stored before jumping.

A base class for RISC-V operations that have one destination register, one source
register and one immediate operand.

This is called I-Type in the RISC-V specification.

In the RISC-V spec, this is the same as `RdRsImmOperation`. For jumps, the `rd` register
is neither an operand, because the stored value is overwritten, nor a result value,
because the value in `rd` is not defined after the jump back. So the `rd` makes the
most sense as an attribute.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V pseudo-instructions that have one destination register and one
source register.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RsRsOffIntegerOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have one source register and a destination
register, and an offset.

This is called B-Type in the RISC-V specification.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RsRsImmIntegerOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have two source registers and an
immediate.

This is called S-Type in the RISC-V specification.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RsRsIntegerOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have two source
registers.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.NullaryOperation
documentation: A base class for RISC-V operations that have neither sources nor destinations.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrReadWriteOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations performing a swap to/from a CSR.

The 'writeonly' attribute controls the actual behaviour of the operation:
* when True, the operation writes the rs value to the CSR but never reads it and
  in this case rd *must* be allocated to x0
* when False, a proper atomic swap is performed and the previous CSR value is
  returned in rd

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrBitwiseOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations performing a masked bitwise operation on the
CSR while returning the original value.

The 'readonly' attribute controls the actual behaviour of the operation:
* when True, the operation is guaranteed to have no side effects that can
  be potentially related to writing to a CSR; in this case rs *must be
  allocated to x0*
* when False, the bitwise operations is performed and any side effect related
  to writing to a CSR takes place even if the mask in rs has no actual bits set.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrReadWriteImmOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations performing a write immediate to/read from a CSR.

The 'writeonly' attribute controls the actual behaviour of the operation:
* when True, the operation writes the rs value to the CSR but never reads it and
  in this case rd *must* be allocated to x0
* when False, a proper atomic swap is performed and the previous CSR value is
  returned in rd

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrBitwiseImmOperation
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations performing a masked bitwise operation on the
CSR while returning the original value. The bitmask is specified in the 'immediate'
attribute.

The 'immediate' attribute controls the actual behaviour of the operation:
* when equals to zero, the operation is guaranteed to have no side effects
  that can be potentially related to writing to a CSR;
* when not equal to zero, any side effect related to writing to a CSR takes
  place.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.AddiOpHasCanonicalizationPatternsTrait
documentation: endregion
region RV32I/RV64I: 2.4 Integer Computational Instructions
# Integer Register-Immediate Instructions

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.AddiOp
documentation:
Adds the sign-extended 12-bit immediate to register rs1.
Arithmetic overflow is ignored and the result is simply the low XLEN bits of the result.

x[rd] = x[rs1] + sext(immediate)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#addi

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SltiOp
documentation:
Place the value 1 in register rd if register rs1 is less than the sign-extended
immediate when both are treated as signed numbers, else 0 is written to rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] <s sext(immediate)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#slti

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SltiuOp
documentation:
Place the value 1 in register rd if register rs1 is less than the immediate when
both are treated as unsigned numbers, else 0 is written to rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] <u sext(immediate)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#sltiu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.AndiOp
documentation:
Performs bitwise AND on register rs1 and the sign-extended 12-bit
immediate and place the result in rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] & sext(immediate)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#andi

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.OriOp
documentation:
Performs bitwise OR on register rs1 and the sign-extended 12-bit immediate and place
the result in rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] | sext(immediate)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#ori

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.XoriOp
documentation:
Performs bitwise XOR on register rs1 and the sign-extended 12-bit immediate and place
the result in rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] ^ sext(immediate)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#xori

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SlliOp
documentation:
Performs logical left shift on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount
held in the lower 5 bits of the immediate.

x[rd] = x[rs1] << shamt

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#slli

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SrliOp
documentation:
Performs logical right shift on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount held
in the lower 5 bits of the immediate.

x[rd] = x[rs1] >>u shamt

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#srli

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SraiOp
documentation:
Performs arithmetic right shift on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount
held in the lower 5 bits of the immediate.

x[rd] = x[rs1] >>s shamt

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#srai

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.LuiOp
documentation:
Build 32-bit constants and uses the U-type format. LUI places the U-immediate value
in the top 20 bits of the destination register rd, filling in the lowest 12 bits with zeros.

x[rd] = sext(immediate[31:12] << 12)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#lui

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.AuipcOp
documentation:
Build pc-relative addresses and uses the U-type format. AUIPC forms a 32-bit offset
from the 20-bit U-immediate, filling in the lowest 12 bits with zeros, adds this
offset to the pc, then places the result in register rd.

x[rd] = pc + sext(immediate[31:12] << 12)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#auipc

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.MVOp
documentation:
A pseudo instruction to copy contents of one int register to another.

Equivalent to `addi rd, rs, 0`

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMVOp
documentation:
A pseudo instruction to copy contents of one float register to another.

Equivalent to `fsgnj.s rd, rs, rs`.

Both clang and gcc emit `fsw rs, 0(x); flw rd, 0(x)` to copy floats, possibly because
storing and loading bits from memory is a lower overhead in practice than reasoning
about floating-point values.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.AddOpHasCanonicalizationPatternsTrait
documentation: # Integer Register-Register Operations

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.AddOp
documentation:
Adds the registers rs1 and rs2 and stores the result in rd.
Arithmetic overflow is ignored and the result is simply the low XLEN bits of the result.

x[rd] = x[rs1] + x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#add

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SltOp
documentation:
Place the value 1 in register rd if register rs1 is less than register rs2 when both
are treated as signed numbers, else 0 is written to rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] <s x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#slt

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SltuOp
documentation:
Place the value 1 in register rd if register rs1 is less than register rs2 when both
are treated as unsigned numbers, else 0 is written to rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] <u x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#sltu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.AndOp
documentation:
Performs bitwise AND on registers rs1 and rs2 and place the result in rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] & x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#and

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.OrOp
documentation:
Performs bitwise OR on registers rs1 and rs2 and place the result in rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] | x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#or

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.XorOp
documentation:
Performs bitwise XOR on registers rs1 and rs2 and place the result in rd.

x[rd] = x[rs1] ^ x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#xor

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SllOp
documentation:
Performs logical left shift on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount
held in the lower 5 bits of register rs2.

x[rd] = x[rs1] << x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#sll

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SrlOp
documentation:
Logical right shift on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount held
in the lower 5 bits of register rs2.

x[rd] = x[rs1] >>u x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#srl

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SubOp
documentation:
Subtracts the registers rs1 and rs2 and stores the result in rd.
Arithmetic overflow is ignored and the result is simply the low XLEN bits of the result.

x[rd] = x[rs1] - x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#sub

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SraOp
documentation:
Performs arithmetic right shift on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount held
in the lower 5 bits of register rs2.

x[rd] = x[rs1] >>s x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#sub

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.NopOp
documentation:
Does not change any user-visible state, except for advancing the pc register.
Canonical nop is encoded as addi x0, x0, 0.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.JalOp
documentation:
Jump to address and place return address in rd.

jal mylabel is a pseudoinstruction for jal ra, mylabel

x[rd] = pc+4; pc += sext(offset)

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#jal
endregion
region RV32I/RV64I: 2.5 Control Transfer Instructions
Unconditional jumps

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.JOp
documentation:
A pseudo-instruction, for unconditional jumps you don't expect to return from.
Is equivalent to JalOp with `rd` = `x0`.
Used to be a part of the spec, removed in 2.0.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.JalrOp
documentation:
Jump to address and place return address in rd.

```
t = pc+4
pc = (x[rs1] + sext(offset)) & ~1
x[rd] = t
```

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#jalr

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.ReturnOp
documentation:
Pseudo-op for returning from subroutine.

Equivalent to `jalr x0, x1, 0`

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.BeqOp
documentation: Conditional Branches

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.BneOp
xdsl.dialects.riscv.BltOp
xdsl.dialects.riscv.BgeOp
xdsl.dialects.riscv.BltuOp
xdsl.dialects.riscv.BgeuOp
xdsl.dialects.riscv.LbOp
documentation:
Loads a 8-bit value from memory and sign-extends this to XLEN bits before
storing it in register rd.

x[rd] = sext(M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)][7:0])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#lb
endregion
region RV32I/RV64I: 2.6 Load and Store Instructions

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.LbuOp
documentation:
Loads a 8-bit value from memory and zero-extends this to XLEN bits before
storing it in register rd.

x[rd] = M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)][7:0]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#lbu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.LhOp
documentation:
Loads a 16-bit value from memory and sign-extends this to XLEN bits before
storing it in register rd.

x[rd] = sext(M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)][15:0])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#lh

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.LhuOp
documentation:
Loads a 16-bit value from memory and zero-extends this to XLEN bits before
storing it in register rd.

x[rd] = M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)][15:0]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#lhu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.LwOp
documentation:
Loads a 32-bit value from memory and sign-extends this to XLEN bits before
storing it in register rd.

x[rd] = sext(M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)][31:0])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#lw

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SbOp
documentation:
Store 8-bit, values from the low bits of register rs2 to memory.

M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)] = x[rs2][7:0]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#sb

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.ShOp
documentation:
Store 16-bit, values from the low bits of register rs2 to memory.

M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)] = x[rs2][15:0]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#sh

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.SwOp
documentation:
Store 32-bit, values from the low bits of register rs2 to memory.

M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)] = x[rs2][31:0]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#sw

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrrwOp
documentation:
Atomically swaps values in the CSRs and integer registers.
CSRRW reads the old value of the CSR, zero-extends the value to XLEN bits,
then writes it to integer register rd. The initial value in rs1 is written
to the CSR. If the 'writeonly' attribute evaluates to False, then the
instruction shall not read the CSR and shall not cause any of the side effects
that might occur on a CSR read; in this case rd *must be allocated to x0*.

t = CSRs[csr]; CSRs[csr] = x[rs1]; x[rd] = t

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#csrrw
endregion
region RV32I/RV64I: 2.8 Control and Status Register Instructions

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrrsOp
documentation:
Reads the value of the CSR, zero-extends the value to XLEN bits, and writes
it to integer register rd. The initial value in integer register rs1 is treated
as a bit mask that specifies bit positions to be set in the CSR.
Any bit that is high in rs1 will cause the corresponding bit to be set in the CSR,
if that CSR bit is writable. Other bits in the CSR are unaffected (though CSRs might
have side effects when written).

If the 'readonly' attribute evaluates to True, then the instruction will not write
to the CSR at all, and so shall not cause any of the side effects that might otherwise
occur on a CSR write, such as raising illegal instruction exceptions on accesses to
read-only CSRs. Note that if rs1 specifies a register holding a zero value other than x0,
the instruction will still attempt to write the unmodified value back to the CSR and will
cause any attendant side effects.

t = CSRs[csr]; CSRs[csr] = t | x[rs1]; x[rd] = t

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#csrrs

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrrcOp
documentation:
Reads the value of the CSR, zero-extends the value to XLEN bits, and writes
it to integer register rd. The initial value in integer register rs1 is treated
as a bit mask that specifies bit positions to be cleared in the CSR.
Any bit that is high in rs1 will cause the corresponding bit to be cleared in the CSR,
if that CSR bit is writable. Other bits in the CSR are unaffected (though CSRs might
have side effects when written).

If the 'readonly' attribute evaluates to True, then the instruction will not write
to the CSR at all, and so shall not cause any of the side effects that might otherwise
occur on a CSR write, such as raising illegal instruction exceptions on accesses to
read-only CSRs. Note that if rs1 specifies a register holding a zero value other than x0,
the instruction will still attempt to write the unmodified value back to the CSR and will
cause any attendant side effects.

t = CSRs[csr]; CSRs[csr] = t &~x[rs1]; x[rd] = t

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#csrrc

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrrwiOp
documentation:
Update the CSR using an XLEN-bit value obtained by zero-extending the
'immediate' attribute.
If the 'writeonly' attribute evaluates to False, then the
instruction shall not read the CSR and shall not cause any of the side effects
that might occur on a CSR read; in this case rd *must be allocated to x0*.

x[rd] = CSRs[csr]; CSRs[csr] = zimm

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#csrrwi

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrrsiOp
documentation:
Reads the value of the CSR, zero-extends the value to XLEN bits, and writes
it to integer register rd. The value in the 'immediate' attribute is treated
as a bit mask that specifies bit positions to be set in the CSR.
Any bit that is high in it will cause the corresponding bit to be set in the CSR,
if that CSR bit is writable. Other bits in the CSR are unaffected (though CSRs might
have side effects when written).

If the 'immediate' attribute value is zero, then the instruction will not write
to the CSR at all, and so shall not cause any of the side effects that might otherwise
occur on a CSR write, such as raising illegal instruction exceptions on accesses to
read-only CSRs.

t = CSRs[csr]; CSRs[csr] = t | zimm; x[rd] = t

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#csrrsi

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CsrrciOp
documentation:
Reads the value of the CSR, zero-extends the value to XLEN bits, and writes
it to integer register rd.  The value in the 'immediate' attribute is treated
as a bit mask that specifies bit positions to be cleared in the CSR.
Any bit that is high in rs1 will cause the corresponding bit to be cleared in the CSR,
if that CSR bit is writable. Other bits in the CSR are unaffected (though CSRs might
have side effects when written).

If the 'immediate' attribute value is zero, then the instruction will not write
to the CSR at all, and so shall not cause any of the side effects that might otherwise
occur on a CSR write, such as raising illegal instruction exceptions on accesses to
read-only CSRs.

t = CSRs[csr]; CSRs[csr] = t &~zimm; x[rd] = t

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#csrrci

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.MulOpHasCanonicalizationPatternsTrait
documentation: endregion
region RV32M/RV64M: 7 “M” Standard Extension for Integer Multiplication and Division
# Multiplication Operations

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.MulOp
documentation:
Performs an XLEN-bit × XLEN-bit multiplication of signed rs1 by signed rs2
and places the lower XLEN bits in the destination register.
x[rd] = x[rs1] * x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvi.html#add

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.MulhOp
documentation:
Performs an XLEN-bit × XLEN-bit multiplication of signed rs1 by signed rs2
and places the upper XLEN bits in the destination register.
x[rd] = (x[rs1] s×s x[rs2]) >>s XLEN

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvm.html#mulh

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.MulhsuOp
documentation:
Performs an XLEN-bit × XLEN-bit multiplication of signed rs1 by unsigned rs2
and places the upper XLEN bits in the destination register.
x[rd] = (x[rs1] s × x[rs2]) >>s XLEN

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvm.html#mulhsu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.MulhuOp
documentation:
Performs an XLEN-bit × XLEN-bit multiplication of unsigned rs1 by unsigned rs2
and places the upper XLEN bits in the destination register.
x[rd] = (x[rs1] u × x[rs2]) >>u XLEN

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvm.html#mulhu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.DivOp
documentation:
Perform an XLEN bits by XLEN bits signed integer division of rs1 by rs2,
rounding towards zero.
x[rd] = x[rs1] /s x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvm.html#div
# Division Operations

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.DivuOp
documentation:
Perform an XLEN bits by XLEN bits unsigned integer division of rs1 by rs2,
rounding towards zero.
x[rd] = x[rs1] /u x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvm.html#divu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RemOp
documentation:
Perform an XLEN bits by XLEN bits signed integer reminder of rs1 by rs2.
x[rd] = x[rs1] %s x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvm.html#rem

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RemuOp
documentation:
Perform an XLEN bits by XLEN bits unsigned integer reminder of rs1 by rs2.
x[rd] = x[rs1] %u x[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvm.html#remu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.LiOpHasCanonicalizationPatternTrait
documentation: endregion
region Assembler pseudo-instructions
https://github.com/riscv-non-isa/riscv-asm-manual/blob/master/riscv-asm.md

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.LiOp
documentation:
Loads a 32-bit immediate into rd.

This is an assembler pseudo-instruction.

https://github.com/riscv-non-isa/riscv-asm-manual/blob/master/riscv-asm.md#load-immediate

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.EcallOp
documentation:
The ECALL instruction is used to make a request to the supporting execution
environment, which is usually an operating system.
The ABI for the system will define how parameters for the environment
request are passed, but usually these will be in defined locations in the
integer register file.

https://github.com/riscv/riscv-isa-manual/releases/download/Ratified-IMAFDQC/riscv-spec-20191213.pdf

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.LabelOp
documentation:
The label operation is used to emit text labels (e.g. loop:) that are used
as branch, unconditional jump targets and symbol offsets.

https://github.com/riscv-non-isa/riscv-asm-manual/blob/master/riscv-asm.md#labels

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.DirectiveOp
documentation:
The directive operation is used to emit assembler directives (e.g. .word; .equ; etc.)
without any associated region of assembly code.
A more complete list of directives can be found here:

https://github.com/riscv-non-isa/riscv-asm-manual/blob/master/riscv-asm.md#pseudo-ops

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.AssemblySectionOp
documentation:
The directive operation is used to emit assembler directives (e.g. .text; .data; etc.)
with the scope of a section.

A more complete list of directives can be found here:

https://github.com/riscv-non-isa/riscv-asm-manual/blob/master/riscv-asm.md#pseudo-ops

This operation can have nested operations, corresponding to a section of the assembly.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.CustomAssemblyInstructionOp
documentation:
An instruction with unspecified semantics, that can be printed during assembly
emission.

During assembly emission, the results are printed before the operands:

``` python
s0 = riscv.GetRegisterOp(Registers.s0).res
s1 = riscv.GetRegisterOp(Registers.s1).res
rs2 = riscv.Registers.s2
rs3 = riscv.Registers.s3
op = CustomAssemblyInstructionOp("my_instr", (s0, s1), (rs2, rs3))

op.assembly_line()   # "my_instr s2, s3, s0, s1"
```

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.EbreakOp
documentation:
The EBREAK instruction is used by debuggers to cause control to be
transferred back to a debugging environment.

https://github.com/riscv/riscv-isa-manual/releases/download/Ratified-IMAFDQC/riscv-spec-20191213.pdf

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.WfiOp
documentation:
The Wait for Interrupt instruction (WFI) provides a hint to the
implementation that the current hart can be stalled until an
interrupt might need servicing.

https://github.com/riscv/riscv-isa-manual/releases/download/Priv-v1.12/riscv-privileged-20211203.pdf

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RegisterAllocatedMemoryEffect
documentation:
An assembly operation that only has side-effect if some registers are allocated to
it.
endregion
region RISC-V SSA Helpers

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.GetAnyRegisterOperation
documentation:
This instruction allows us to create an SSAValue with for a given register name. This
is useful for bridging the RISC-V convention that stores the result of function calls
in `a0` and `a1` into SSA form.

For example, to generate this assembly:
```
jal my_func
add a0 s0 a0
```

One needs to do the following:

``` python
rhs = riscv.GetRegisterOp(Registers.s0).res
riscv.JalOp("my_func")
lhs = riscv.GetRegisterOp(Registers.A0).res
sum = riscv.AddOp(lhs, rhs, Registers.A0).rd
```

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsRsRsFloatOperation
documentation:
A base class for RV32F operations that take three
floating-point input registers and a destination register,
e.g: fused-multiply-add (FMA) instructions.
endregion
region RV32F: 8 “F” Standard Extension for Single-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.0

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsRsFloatFloatIntegerOperation
documentation:
A base class for RV32F operations that take
two floating-point input registers and an integer destination register.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsRsFloatFloatIntegerOperationWithFastMath
documentation:
A base class for RISC-V operations that have two source floating-point
registers with an integer destination register, and can be annotated with fastmath flags.

This is called R-Type in the RISC-V specification.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RsRsImmFloatOperation
documentation:
A base class for RV32F operations that have two source registers
(one integer and one floating-point) and an immediate.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.RdRsImmFloatOperation
documentation:
A base class for RV32Foperations that have one floating-point
destination register, one source register and
one immediate operand.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMAddSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision fused multiply addition.

f[rd] = f[rs1]×f[rs2]+f[rs3]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmadd-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMSubSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision fused multiply substraction.

f[rd] = f[rs1]×f[rs2]+f[rs3]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmsub-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FNMSubSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision fused multiply substraction.

f[rd] = -f[rs1]×f[rs2]+f[rs3]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fnmsub-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FNMAddSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision fused multiply addition.

f[rd] = -f[rs1]×f[rs2]-f[rs3]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fnmadd-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FAddSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision floating-point addition.

f[rd] = f[rs1]+f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fadd-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FSubSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision floating-point substraction.

f[rd] = f[rs1]-f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fsub-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMulSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision floating-point multiplication.

f[rd] = f[rs1]×f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmul-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FDivSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision floating-point division.

f[rd] = f[rs1] / f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fdiv-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FSqrtSOp
documentation:
Perform single-precision floating-point square root.

f[rd] = sqrt(f[rs1])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fsqrt-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FSgnJSOp
documentation:
Produce a result that takes all bits except the sign bit from rs1.
The result’s sign bit is rs2’s sign bit.

f[rd] = {f[rs2][31], f[rs1][30:0]}

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fsgnj.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FSgnJNSOp
documentation:
Produce a result that takes all bits except the sign bit from rs1.
The result’s sign bit is opposite of rs2’s sign bit.


f[rd] = {~f[rs2][31], f[rs1][30:0]}

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fsgnjn.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FSgnJXSOp
documentation:
Produce a result that takes all bits except the sign bit from rs1.
The result’s sign bit is XOR of sign bit of rs1 and rs2.

f[rd] = {f[rs1][31] ^ f[rs2][31], f[rs1][30:0]}

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fsgnjx.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMinSOp
documentation:
Write the smaller of single precision data in rs1 and rs2 to rd.

f[rd] = min(f[rs1], f[rs2])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmin-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMaxSOp
documentation:
Write the larger of single precision data in rs1 and rs2 to rd.

f[rd] = max(f[rs1], f[rs2])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmax-s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FCvtWSOp
documentation:
Convert a floating-point number in floating-point register rs1 to a signed 32-bit in integer register rd.

x[rd] = sext(s32_{f32}(f[rs1]))

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fcvt.w.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FCvtWuSOp
documentation:
Convert a floating-point number in floating-point register rs1 to a signed 32-bit in unsigned integer register rd.

x[rd] = sext(u32_{f32}(f[rs1]))

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fcvt.wu.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMvXWOp
documentation:
Move the single-precision value in floating-point register rs1 represented in IEEE 754-2008 encoding to the lower 32 bits of integer register rd.

x[rd] = sext(f[rs1][31:0])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmv.x.w

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FeqSOp
documentation:
Performs a quiet equal comparison between floating-point registers rs1 and rs2 and record the Boolean result in integer register rd.
Only signaling NaN inputs cause an Invalid Operation exception.
The result is 0 if either operand is NaN.

x[rd] = f[rs1] == f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#feq.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FltSOp
documentation:
Performs a quiet less comparison between floating-point registers rs1 and rs2 and record the Boolean result in integer register rd.
Only signaling NaN inputs cause an Invalid Operation exception.
The result is 0 if either operand is NaN.

x[rd] = f[rs1] < f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#flt.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FleSOp
documentation:
Performs a quiet less or equal comparison between floating-point registers rs1 and rs2 and record the Boolean result in integer register rd.
Only signaling NaN inputs cause an Invalid Operation exception.
The result is 0 if either operand is NaN.

x[rd] = f[rs1] <= f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fle.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FClassSOp
documentation:
Examines the value in floating-point register rs1 and writes to integer register rd a 10-bit mask that indicates the class of the floating-point number.
The format of the mask is described in [classify table]_.
The corresponding bit in rd will be set if the property is true and clear otherwise.
All other bits in rd are cleared. Note that exactly one bit in rd will be set.

x[rd] = classifys(f[rs1])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fclass.s

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FCvtSWOp
documentation:
Converts a 32-bit signed integer, in integer register rs1 into a floating-point number in floating-point register rd.

f[rd] = f32_{s32}(x[rs1])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fcvt.s.w

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FCvtSWuOp
documentation:
Converts a 32-bit unsigned integer, in integer register rs1 into a floating-point number in floating-point register rd.

f[rd] = f32_{u32}(x[rs1])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fcvt.s.wu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMvWXOp
documentation:
Move the single-precision value encoded in IEEE 754-2008 standard encoding from the lower 32 bits of integer register rs1 to the floating-point register rd.

f[rd] = x[rs1][31:0]


https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmv.w.x

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FLwOp
documentation:
Load a single-precision value from memory into floating-point register rd.

f[rd] = M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)][31:0]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#flw

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FSwOp
documentation:
Store a single-precision value from floating-point register rs2 to memory.

M[x[rs1] + offset] = f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fsw

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMAddDOp
documentation:
Perform double-precision fused multiply addition.

f[rd] = f[rs1]×f[rs2]+f[rs3]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmadd-d
endregion
region RV32F: 9 “D” Standard Extension for Double-Precision Floating-Point, Version 2.0

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMSubDOp
documentation:
Perform double-precision fused multiply substraction.

f[rd] = f[rs1]×f[rs2]+f[rs3]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmsub-d

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FAddDOp
documentation:
Perform double-precision floating-point addition.

f[rd] = f[rs1]+f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fadd-d

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FSubDOp
documentation:
Perform double-precision floating-point substraction.

f[rd] = f[rs1]-f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fsub-d

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMulDOp
documentation:
Perform double-precision floating-point multiplication.

f[rd] = f[rs1]×f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmul-d

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FDivDOp
documentation:
Perform double-precision floating-point division.

f[rd] = f[rs1] / f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fdiv-d

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMinDOp
documentation:
Write the smaller of double precision data in rs1 and rs2 to rd.

f[rd] = min(f[rs1], f[rs2])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmin-d

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMaxDOp
documentation:
Write the larger of single precision data in rs1 and rs2 to rd.

f[rd] = max(f[rs1], f[rs2])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fmax-d

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FCvtDWOp
documentation:
Converts a 32-bit signed integer, in integer register rs1 into a double-precision floating-point number in floating-point register rd.

x[rd] = sext(s32_{f64}(f[rs1]))

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fcvt-d-w

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FCvtDWuOp
documentation:
Converts a 32-bit unsigned integer, in integer register rs1 into a double-precision floating-point number in floating-point register rd.

f[rd] = f64_{u32}(x[rs1])

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fcvt-d-wu

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FLdOp
documentation:
Load a double-precision value from memory into floating-point register rd.

f[rd] = M[x[rs1] + sext(offset)][63:0]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fld

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FSdOp
documentation:
Store a double-precision value from floating-point register rs2 to memory.

M[x[rs1] + offset] = f[rs2]

https://msyksphinz-self.github.io/riscv-isadoc/html/rvfd.html#fsw

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.FMvDOp
documentation:
A pseudo instruction to copy 64 bits of one float register to another.

Equivalent to `fsgnj.d rd, rs, rs`.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.VFAddSOp
documentation:
Perform a pointwise single-precision floating-point addition over vectors.

If the registers used are FloatRegisterType, they must be 64-bit wide, and contain two
32-bit single-precision floating point values.
endregion
region 17 "V" Standard Extension for Vector Operations
https://riscv.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/05/15.20-15.55-18.05.06.VEXT-bcn-v1.pdf
Vector operations that use standard RISC-V registers are using a non-standard Xfvec
extension.
All Xfvec instructions performing vectorial single precision operations require 64bit
floating point registers (a.k.a.: FLEN==64).
https://iis-git.ee.ethz.ch/smach/smallFloat-spec/-/raw/master/smallFloat_isa.pdf

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.riscv.VFMulSOp
documentation:
Perform a pointwise single-precision floating-point multiplication over vectors.

If the registers used are FloatRegisterType, they must be 64-bit wide, and contain two
32-bit single-precision floating point values.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_stencil.ExchangeDeclarationAttr
documentation:
A simplified version of dmp.exchange, from which it should be lowered.

`neighbor` is a list (e.g. [x, y]) where
  - the index encodes dimension,
  - the sign encodes direction,
  - the magnitude encodes distance
As such, the values correspond to those used by both stencil.access and dmp.exchange

This works irrespective of whether the accesses are diagonal or not.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_stencil.PrefetchOp
documentation:
An op to indicate a symmetric (send and receive) buffer prefetch across the stencil shape.

This should be irrespective of the stencil shape (and whether it does or does not include diagonals).

Returns memref<${len(self.swaps}xtensor<{buffer size}x{data type}>>

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_stencil.ApplyOp

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_stencil.ApplyOp.get_accesses
documentation:
Return the access patterns of each input.

 - An offset is a tuple describing a relative access
 - An access pattern is a class wrapping a sequence of offsets
 - This method returns an access pattern for each stencil
   field of the apply operation.

This operation combines a `csl_stencil.prefetch` (symmetric buffer communication across a given stencil shape)
with a `stencil.apply` (a stencil function plus parameters and applies the stencil function to the output temp).

As communication may be done in chunks, this operation provides two regions for computation:
  - the `receive_chunk` region to reduce a chunk of data received from several neighbours to one chunk of data.
    this region is invoked once per communicated chunks and effectively acts as a loop body.
    It uses `accumulator` to concatenate the chunks
  - the `done_exchange` region (invoked once when communication has finished) that takes the concatenated
    chunk of the `receive_chunk` region and applies any further processing here - for instance, it may handle
    the computation of 'own' (non-communicated) or otherwise prefetched data

Further fields:
  - `field`      - the stencil field to communicate (send and receive)
  - `args_rchunk`  - arguments passed to the `receive_chunk` region, may include other prefetched buffers
  - `args_dexchng` - arguments passed to the `done_exchange` region, may include other prefetched buffers
  - `args`       - arguments to the stencil computation, may include other prefetched buffers
  - `topo`       - as received from `csl_stencil.prefetch`/`dmp.swap`
  - `num_chunks` - number of chunks into which to slice the communication
  - `swaps`      - a set of neighbouring points in the stencil, whose value we wish to retain
                   (note, these are not guaranteed to be lowered as true point-to-point communication, and
                   redundant communication should be irgnored)

Function signatures:
Before lowering (from `csl_stencil.prefetch` and `stencil.apply`):
    %pref = csl_stencil.prefetch(%field : stencil.Temp)
    stencil.apply( ..some args.. , %field, ..some more args.., %pref)

After lowering:
    op:             csl_stencil.apply(%field, %accumulator, receive_chunk_args..., done_exchange_args...)
    receive_chunk:   block_args(slice of type(%pref), %offset, %accumulator, args...)
    done_exchange:   block_args(%field, %accumulator, args...)

Note, that %pref can be dropped (as communication is done by the op rather than before the op),
and that a new %accumulator is required, an empty tensor which is filled by `receive_chunk` and
consumed by `done_exchange`

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_stencil.AccessOp

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_stencil.AccessOp.get_apply
documentation: Simple helper to get the parent apply and raise otherwise.

A CSL stencil access that operates on own data or data prefetched from neighbors via `csl_stencil.prefetch`

The source of data determines the type `op` is required to have:

  ${type(op) == memref.MemRefType}  -  for accesses to data prefetched from neighbors
  ${type(op) == stencil.Temp}       -  for accesses to own data

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ParamAttribute
documentation: Represents a module parameter that needs to have a type, and may have a value.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ImportOp
documentation:
Lightweight wrapper around `csl.import_module` that allows specifying field names directly
and removes the need for handling structs or setting up struct operands.

Where existing structs need to be used in the import, they can be passed
with an empty field name. This will concatenate them all together.

Named fields and empty fields can be used in the same import

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ModuleOp

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ModuleOp.update_program_block_args
documentation:
Update `program_module` BlockArguments by adding
1. yield op fields (pass None to enable automated retrieval, pass empty list to add no yield op fields)
2. additional exported symbols

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ModuleOp.get_layout_param
documentation: Retrieve layout block arg for name that is x, y, or one of the properties

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ModuleOp.get_program_param
documentation: Retrieve program block arg for name that is one of the properties or a param set up by layout yield

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ModuleOp.get_param_value
documentation: Retrieve the value of a named op param.

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ModuleOp.layout_yield_op
documentation: Get the yield op from the layout module. Used in various places.

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ModuleOp.exported_symbols
documentation: Get the exported symbols.

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.ModuleOp.get_program_import
documentation: Get top-level import op in the program_module

Wrapper class that manages initialisation of layout and program module.

Specified properties will be lowered to module params (`csl.param`). As such, all properties
are passed as BlockArgs to the `layout_module` and `program_module` region. The order in which
properties are specified is important and must match the order of the block args.

Additionally, any value yielded by the `layout_module` is passed as a block arg (lowered to `csl.param`)
to the program module. Order is important here as well. The layout module's `yield` op should be lowered to
`@set_tile_code`, while the program module's `yield` op should be discarded.

The layout module has two additional block args `x` and `y` as part of the `@set_tile_code` loop nest.
Operations using these args need to be lowered to the correct place in the loop nest.

The layout module has the following args (in order):
  * set_tile_code params:  `x` and `y`
  * general params:        `width` and `height` followed by everything specified in `params`

The program module has the following args (in order):
  * general params:        `width` and `height` followed by everything specified in `params`
  * params from layout:    everything defined by `layout_yield_op.fields`

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl_wrapper.YieldOp
documentation:
Layout module `yield` ops should be lowered to `@set_tile_code` and must specify `fields`.
Program module `yield` ops have no particular meaning and specify `fields` here is permitted but undefined.

modules.name: xdsl.dialects.ltl
documentation:
Implementation of the LTL dialect by CIRCT.

https://circt.llvm.org/docs/Dialects/LTL/

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.ltl.Property
documentation:
The `ltl.property` type represents a verifiable property built from linear
temporal logic sequences and quantifiers, for example,
"if you see sequence A, eventually you will see sequence B".

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.ltl.Sequence
documentation:
The ltl.sequence type represents a sequence of linear temporal logic, for example,
“A is true two cycles after B is true”.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.ltl.AndOp
documentation:
A conjunction of booleans, sequences, or properties. If any of the `inputs` is of type
!ltl.property or !ltl.sequence or 1-bit signless int, the result of the op is consistent with the input type.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.EffectsEnum
documentation:
Specify to explicitly say:
* NONE: There are no accfg side-effects to this operation
* FULL: There are accfg side-effects to this operation

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.EffectsAttr
documentation: An Attribute specifying if the marked operation has any effects on accelerator states.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.TokenType
documentation: Async token type for launched accelerator requests.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.StateType
documentation: Used to trace an accelerators CSR state through def-use chain

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.LaunchOp

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.LaunchOp.values
documentation: The actual values used to set up registers linked to launch
TODO: make more precise?

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.LaunchOp.param_names
documentation: Maps the SSA values in `values` to accelerator launch parameters

Launch an accelerator. This acts as a barrier for CSR values,
meaning CSRs can be safely modified after a launch op without
interfering with the Accelerator.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.AwaitOp
documentation: Blocks until the launched operation finishes.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.SetupOp

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.SetupOp.values
documentation: The actual values used to set up the CSRs
TODO: make more precise?

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.SetupOp.in_state
documentation: The state produced by a previous accfg.setup

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.SetupOp.out_state
documentation: The CSR state after the setup op modified it.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.SetupOp.param_names
documentation: Maps the SSA values in `values` to accelerator parameter names

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.SetupOp.accelerator
documentation: Name of the accelerator this setup is for

accfg.setup writes values to a specific accelerators configuration and returns
a value representing the currently known state of that accelerator's config.

If accfg.setup is called without any parameters, the resulting state is the
"empty" state, that represents a state without known values.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.AcceleratorOp

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.accfg.AcceleratorOp.barrier
documentation: TODO: this will be reworked in a later version

Declares an accelerator that can be configured, launched, etc.
`fields` is a dictionary mapping accelerator configuration names to
CSR addresses.

modules.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream
documentation:
A target-independent representation of streams of buffers over time.

Currently a higher-level representation of the `snitch_stream` dialect, operating on
memrefs instead of registers storing pointers.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.IteratorType.PARALLEL
documentation: The corresponding iterators appear in the output.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.IteratorType.REDUCTION
documentation: The corresponding iterators do not appear in the output.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.IteratorType.INTERLEAVED
documentation:
All inputs and outputs of the operation will be operated this many times in parallel.
This is helpful to circumvent the latency in the loop.
For example, if the ALU of the target has a pipeline of length 4, and the operation
accumulates its innermost dimension, there will be stalls waiting fof the pipeline to
clear in each iteration.
By interleaving the loop with a factor of 4, four dimensions can be processed in
parallel, removing the stalls.
The corresponding iterators may appear in the output.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.StridePattern
xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.StreamingRegionOp

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.StreamingRegionOp.inputs
documentation:
Pointers to memory buffers that will be streamed. The corresponding stride pattern
defines the order in which the elements of the input buffers will be read.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.StreamingRegionOp.outputs
documentation:
Pointers to memory buffers that will be streamed. The corresponding stride pattern
defines the order in which the elements of the input buffers will be written to.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.StreamingRegionOp.patterns
documentation:
Stride patterns that define the order of the input and output streams.
Like in linalg.generic, the indexing maps corresponding to inputs are followed by the
indexing maps for the outputs.

An operation that creates streams from access patterns, which are only available to
read from and write to within the body of the operation.

Within the loop body, memrefs that are streamed must not be otherwise accessed
via memref.load, memref.store or any other access means, including extraction (e.g.: memref.view).

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.GenericOp.get_static_loop_ranges
documentation:
This operation can represent two sets of perfectly nested loops, or one.
If it is one, then the first element of the returned tuple has all the loop
bounds, and the second is empty.
If there are two, then the first element of the returned tuple has the outer
bounds, and the second the inner.
Interleaved iterators are not returned in either tuple.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.GenericOp.inputs
documentation:
Pointers to memory buffers or streams to be operated on. The corresponding stride
pattern defines the order in which the elements of the input buffers will be read.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.GenericOp.outputs
documentation:
Pointers to memory buffers or streams to be operated on. The corresponding stride
pattern defines the order in which the elements of the input buffers will be written
to.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.GenericOp.inits
documentation:
Initial values for outputs. The outputs are at corresponding `init_indices`. The inits
may be set only for the imperfectly nested form.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.GenericOp.indexing_maps
documentation:
Stride patterns that define the order of the input and output streams.
Like in linalg.generic, the indexing maps corresponding to inputs are followed by the
indexing maps for the outputs.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.GenericOp.bounds
documentation: The bounds of the iteration space, from the outermost loop inwards. All indexing maps must have the same number of dimensions as the length of `bounds`.

class_variables.name: xdsl.dialects.memref_stream.GenericOp.init_indices
documentation: Indices into the `outputs` that correspond to the initial values in `inits`.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.tensor.CastOp
documentation:
Convert a tensor from one type to an equivalent type without changing any data elements.
The source and destination types must both be tensor types with the same element type.
If both are ranked, then the rank should be the same and static dimensions should match.
The operation is invalid if converting to a mismatching constant dimension.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.tensor.DimOp
documentation:
The tensor.dim operation takes a tensor and a dimension operand of type index.
It returns the size of the requested dimension of the given tensor.
If the dimension index is out of bounds, the behavior is undefined

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.tensor.ExtractOp.__init__
documentation: assembly_format = "$tensor `[` $indices `]` attr-dict `:` type($tensor)"

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.tensor.InsertOp.__init__
documentation: assembly_format = "$scalar `into` $dest `[` $indices `]` attr-dict `:` type($dest)"

modules.name: xdsl.dialects.seq
documentation:
CIRCT’s seq dialect

[1] https://circt.llvm.org/docs/Dialects/Seq/

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.seq.ClockType
documentation: A type for clock-carrying wires. Signals which can be used to drive the clock input of sequential operations.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.seq.ClockDividerOp
documentation: Produces a clock divided by a power of two

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.seq.CompRegOp
documentation: Register a value, storing it for one cycle.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.seq.ClockConstAttr
documentation:
Clock constant.

This attribute diverges slightly from the upstream implementation
as xDSL does not allow completely unstructured parsing and printing
of attributes (for good reasons).

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.seq.ConstClockOp
documentation: The constant operation produces a constant clock value.

modules.name: xdsl.dialects.eqsat
documentation:
An embedding of equivalence classes in IR, for use in equality saturation with
non-destructive rewrites.

Please see the Equality Saturation Project for details:
https://github.com/orgs/xdslproject/projects/23

TODO: add documentation once we have end-to-end flow working:
https://github.com/xdslproject/xdsl/issues/3174

variables.name: xdsl.dialects.eqsat.EQSAT_COST_LABEL
documentation: Key used to store the cost of computing the result of an operation.

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.scf.WhileOp.verify_
documentation: TODO verify dependencies between scf.condition, scf.yield and the regions

modules.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl
documentation:
The CSL dialect models the Cerebras Systems Language. It's meant to be used as a target to do automatic codegen for
the CS2.

See https://docs.cerebras.net/en/latest/ for some mediocre documentation on the operations and their semantics.

This is meant to be used in conjunction with the `-t csl` printing option to generate CSL code.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl._FuncBase
documentation: Base class for the shared functionalty of FuncOp and TaskOp

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.InModuleKind
documentation:
Constrain an op to a particular module kind

Optionally specify if the op has to be a direct child of CslModuleOp
(default is yes).

Ops with this trait are always allowed inside a csl_wrapper.module

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.ComptimeStructType
documentation:
Represents a compile time struct.

The type makes no guarantees on the fields available.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.ImportedModuleType
documentation:
Represents an imported module (behaves the same as a comptime_struct otherwise).

The type makes no guarantees on the fields available.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.PtrKindAttr
documentation: Attribute representing whether a pointer is a single (*) or many ([*]) pointer

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.PtrConstAttr
documentation: Attribute representing whether a pointer's mutability

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.ModuleKindAttr
documentation: Attribute representing the kind of CSL module, either layout or program

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.PtrType
documentation:
Represents a typed pointer in CSL.

kind refers to CSL having two types of pointers, single `*type` and many `[*]type`.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.PtrCastOp
documentation: Implements `@ptrcast(destination_ptr_type, ptr)`

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.DsdType
documentation: Represents a DSD in CSL.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.ColorType
documentation: Type representing a `color` type in CSL

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.VariableOp
documentation:
Declares a variable.

The variable cannot be mutated directly. SSA values of the variable have to
be loaded and stored using LoadVarOp and StoreVarOp.

This is similar to how `memref` works.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.LoadVarOp
documentation:
Obtain the SSA value of a CSL variable. The obtained value itself is not
modifiable, but it can be stored in the variable using `StoreVarOp`.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.StoreVarOp
documentation: Update the value of a variable.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.CslModuleOp
documentation: Separates layout module from program module

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.ImportModuleConstOp
documentation: Equivalent to an `const <va_name> = @import_module("<module_name>", <params>)` call.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.ZerosOp
documentation: Represents the @zeros operation in CSL.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.ConstantsOp
documentation:
Represents the @constants operation in CSL.

This can also be used as a stand-in for @zeros by passing a zero constant as the second argument.

If is_const is present, it is printed with the `const` prefix, otherwise it's assumed `var` by the csl printer.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.MemberAccessOp
documentation: Access a member of a struct and assigna a new variable.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.MemberCallOp
documentation: Call a member of a struct, optionally assign a value to the result.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.FuncOp
documentation:
Almost the same as func.func, but only has one result, and is not isolated from above.

We dropped IsolatedFromAbove because CSL functions often times access global parameters
or constants.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.TaskOp
documentation:
Represents a task in CSL. All three types of task are represented by this Op.

It carries the ID it should be bound to, in case of local and control tasks
this is the task ID, in the case of the data task, it's the id of the color
the task is bound to.

NOTE: Control tasks not yet implemented

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.ActivateOp
documentation:
This operation corresponds directly to the builtin `@activate` combined with a call to the
corresponding `@get_<kind>_task_id` to convert the numeric ID to a task id, e.g.:

```
csl.activate local, 0 : i32
       |
       V
@activate(@get_local_task_id(0));

```

Returns a clone of the DSD with a different base_addr.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.IncrementDsdOffsetOp
documentation:
Returns a clone of the DSD with a different offset
Only works on memory DSDs, i.e. mem1d_dsd or mem4d_dsd.

Implements the CSL built-in
@increment_dsd_offset(input_dsd, offset, elem_type)

where offset is a 16-bit signed int that may be negative,
and elem_type is used to convert offset into words (any u,i,f type of 16,32 bit)
elem_type should be derived by the printer

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.SetDsdLengthOp

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.csl.csl.SetDsdStrideOp

It corresponds to @export_name in layout and @export_symbol in program.

This op comes in two modes:

  * var_name: SymbolRefAttr, type: FunctionType, value: None

Is this operation a gate?
Qref gates represent standard quantum logic gates.

modules.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo
documentation:
https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md

StableHLO is an operation set for high-level operations (HLO) in machine learning (ML) models.
StableHLO works as a portability layer between different ML frameworks and ML compilers:
ML frameworks that produce StableHLO programs are compatible with ML compilers that consume StableHLO programs.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.ElementwiseBinaryOperation
documentation: region Abstract Base Classes

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.Precision
documentation: XLA precision for an operand. Has backend specific meaning.
endregion
region Attributes

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.PrecisionAttr
documentation:
XLA precision for an operand. Has backend specific meaning.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/b075e948092d8a27ed0be48f4f8dbaa6df7e2e3e/stablehlo/dialect/StablehloEnums.td#L46

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.TokenType
xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.DotAttr
documentation:
Attribute that models the dimension information for dot.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/b075e948092d8a27ed0be48f4f8dbaa6df7e2e3e/stablehlo/dialect/StablehloAttrs.td#L82

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.AbsOp
documentation:
Performs element-wise abs operation on operand tensor and produces a result tensor.
Depending on the element type, does the following:

* For signed integers: integer modulus.
* For floats: abs from IEEE-754.
* For complex numbers: complex modulus.
* For quantized types: dequantize_op_quantize(abs, operand, type(result)).

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md#abs
endregion

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.AddOp
documentation:
Performs element-wise addition of two tensors `lhs` and `rhs` and produces a
`result` tensor. Depending on the element type, does the following:

* For booleans: logical OR.
* For integers: integer addition.
* For floats: `addition` from IEEE-754.
* For complex numbers: complex addition.
* For quantized types: `dequantize_op_quantize(add, lhs, rhs, type(result))`.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md#add

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.AfterAllOp
documentation:
Ensures that the operations producing the inputs are executed before any operations that depend on result.
Execution of this operation does nothing, it only exists to establish data dependencies from result to inputs.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md#after_all

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.AndOp
documentation:
Performs element-wise AND of two tensors lhs and rhs and produces a result tensor. Depending on the element type, does the following:

For booleans: logical AND.
For integers: bitwise AND.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md#and

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.CaseOp
documentation:
Semantics

Produces the output from executing exactly one function from branches depending on the value of index.
More formally, result = selected_branch() where:

selected_branch = branches[index] if 0 <= index < size(branches).
selected_branch = branches[-1] otherwise.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md#case

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.BitcastConvertOp
documentation:
Performs a bitcast operation on operand tensor and produces a result tensor
where the bits of the entire operand tensor are reinterpreted using the type of the result tensor.

More formally, given E = element_type(operand), E' = element_type(result), and R = rank(operand):

If num_bits(E') < num_bits(E), bits(result[i0, ..., iR-1, :]) = bits(operand[i0, ..., iR-1]).
If num_bits(E') > num_bits(E), bits(result[i0, ..., iR-2]) = bits(operand[i0, ..., iR-2, :]).
If num_bits(E') = num_bits(E), bits(result[i0, ..., iR-1]) = bits(operand[i0, ..., iR-1]).

bits returns in-memory representation of a given value,
and its behavior is implementation-defined because the exact representation of tensors is implementation-defined,
and the exact representation of element types is implementation-defined as well.

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.MultiplyOp
documentation:
Performs element-wise product of two tensors `lhs` and `rhs` and produces a
`result` tensor. Depending on the element type, does the following:

* For booleans: logical AND.
* For integers: integer multiplication.
* For floats: `multiplication` from IEEE-754.
* For complex numbers: complex multiplication.
* For quantized types:
* `dequantize_op_quantize(multiply, lhs, rhs, type(result))`.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md#multiply

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.SubtractOp
documentation:
Performs element-wise subtraction of two tensors `lhs` and `rhs` and produces a
`result` tensor. Depending on the element type, does the following:

* For integers: integer subtraction.
* For floats: `subtraction` from IEEE-754.
* For complex numbers: complex subtraction.
* For quantized types:
* `dequantize_op_quantize(subtract, lhs, rhs, type(result))`.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md#subtract

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.ReturnOp
documentation:
This op is un-documented.

StableHLO's return is used inside of the bodies of StableHLO ops.
It behaves like func.return but for StableHLO ops.
The func.return op is used inside of func.func op.

https://discord.com/channels/999073994483433573/1259494021269688360/1259992088565645312

classes.name: xdsl.dialects.stablehlo.TransposeOp
documentation:
Permutes the dimensions of `operand` tensor using `permutation` and produces a
`result` tensor. More formally, `result[result_index] = operand[operand_index]`
where `result_index[d] = operand_index[permutation[d]]`.

https://github.com/openxla/stablehlo/blob/main/docs/spec.md#transpose

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.stencil.IndexAttr.parse_parameters
documentation: Parse the attribute parameters.

methods.name: xdsl.dialects.stencil.IndexAttr.parse_nested_parameters
documentation: Parse only the attribute parameters without enclosing angle brackets.
